name: Mastra
description: TypeScript-first AI agent framework from the Gatsby founders, combining agents, durable workflows, RAG, and evals in one toolkit. Provider-agnostic via Vercel AI SDK model routing, with a local dev playground and 1.0 stable release in January 2026.

evidence.value: Agents combine tool calling, memory, and structured workflows; built-in evals allow teams to measure task accuracy

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Task completion testing across agent and workflow primitives

evidence.value: Zod-schema typed tools with input validation plus MCP client/server support reduce malformed tool calls

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Tool invocation testing with typed schemas and MCP servers

evidence.value: Graph-based durable workflows with branching, parallel steps, suspend/resume, and human-in-the-loop

multi_step_planning.methodology: Complex multi-step task testing using workflow engine

evidence.value: Built-in working memory, conversation history, and semantic recall backed by pluggable storage adapters

memory_persistence.methodology: Memory system evaluation across threads and semantic recall

evidence.value: Workflow steps support retries, error branches, and durable suspend/resume after failures

error_recovery.methodology: Error injection testing on workflow retry and resume paths

evidence.value: Agents composable as workflow steps and sub-agents; agent network primitives for dynamic routing between agents

agent_collaboration.methodology: Multi-agent coordination testing via sub-agents and networks

evidence.value: No built-in tool sandboxing; tools run in the host Node.js process and isolation is the developer's responsibility

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of tool execution model

evidence.value: Auth middleware hooks on the Mastra server; RBAC and SSO are enterprise features rather than core defaults

access_control.methodology: Access control assessment of server auth options

evidence.value: Input/output processors enable moderation and PII filtering guardrails, but injection defense is not on by default

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Injection testing with and without guardrail processors configured

evidence.value: Memory and storage scoped per thread/resource with pluggable backends; multi-tenant isolation is application-level

data_isolation.methodology: Data isolation architecture review of storage and memory scoping

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 core with 22k+ stars; enterprise features are source-available under the Mastra Enterprise License

open_source_transparency.methodology: Source code and license structure review

evidence.value: Framework runs in user infrastructure with user-chosen storage backends; full control of retention when self-hosted

data_retention.methodology: Privacy architecture review of self-hosted deployment

evidence.value: GDPR compliance achievable through self-hosting and storage choice; no turnkey compliance tooling in core

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance capabilities assessment of deployment configurations

evidence.value: Data flows to whichever provider is configured via Vercel AI SDK routing; local models supported through Ollama-compatible providers

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis across model provider configurations

evidence.value: Deploys as a standard Node.js server anywhere, including fully self-hosted environments with local model providers
Polished docs with guides, templates, course content, and examples; quality reflects Gatsby founders' DX background
OpenTelemetry tracing of agent runs and workflow steps plus a local playground for inspecting executions
Local studio visualizes agent steps, tool calls, and workflow graphs, aiding inspection of decisions

decision_explainability.methodology: Explainability assessment of run visualization features

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 core, 1.0 stable released 2026-01-21; some enterprise modules under source-available Mastra Enterprise License

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment of license split and code availability

evidence.value: 22k+ GitHub stars, 300k+ weekly npm downloads, active Discord and rapid release cadence
Release cadence remains rapid post-1.0: mastra 1.15.1 and @mastra/core 1.50.0 published within a day of 2026-07-09; downloads grew to ~1.8M/month by Feb 2026

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis of stars, downloads, and releases

evidence.value: create-mastra CLI scaffolds a typed project with playground in minutes; idiomatic TypeScript APIs

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration complexity assessment with scaffolded project testing

evidence.value: Deploys to serverless platforms (Vercel, Cloudflare, Netlify) and Node servers; durable workflows support long-running jobs
Free Apache 2.0 core; costs from LLM APIs, hosting, and optional Mastra Cloud/enterprise offerings
Built-in evals (faithfulness, hallucination, relevance scorers) plus OTel export to external observability platforms
1.0 stable shipped 2026-01-21 with semver commitment after a year of rapid pre-1.0 iteration

production_readiness.methodology: Production readiness assessment of API stability and release maturity

code-generation.notes: Typed TypeScript tooling and MCP support fit developer-workflow agents well

customer-support.notes: Memory, RAG, workflow human-in-the-loop, and guardrail processors suit support bots

content-creation.notes: Workflows plus evals enable quality-controlled content pipelines

research-assistant.notes: RAG primitives and agent networks handle multi-source research tasks

data-analysis.notes: Capable via tools and workflows, though Python data ecosystem is unavailable

education.notes: Memory threads and structured workflows work well for tutoring applications

best_for: TypeScript/JavaScript teams wanting a full-stack agent framework with strong DX
Products needing durable workflows with human-in-the-loop and suspend/resume
Teams that want agents, RAG, memory, and evals in one integrated toolkit
Startups deploying agents to serverless platforms like Vercel or Cloudflare
strengths: TypeScript-first with end-to-end type safety via Zod schemas
Integrated suite: agents, durable workflows, RAG, memory, and evals in one framework
Provider-agnostic model routing through the Vercel AI SDK
Excellent developer experience: CLI scaffolding, local playground, polished docs
1.0 stable (Jan 2026) with strong adoption: 22k+ stars, 300k+ weekly npm downloads
Built-in eval scorers and OpenTelemetry observability
limitations: No built-in tool sandboxing; tools execute in the host Node.js process
Enterprise features (RBAC, SSO, some modules) are source-available, not Apache 2.0
Young 1.0; some advanced patterns and integrations still maturing
No Python SDK, limiting access to the Python ML/data ecosystem
Security guardrails (processors) are opt-in rather than default

metadata.license: Apache 2.0 (core); Mastra Enterprise License (source-available enterprise features)
supported_models: Any provider via Vercel AI SDK routing
metadata.pricing: Free (Apache 2.0 core) - Costs from LLM APIs, hosting, and optional enterprise/cloud offerings
metadata.adoption: 300k+ weekly npm downloads; built by the Gatsby founders
